title: rubus nemorosus subsp. serrulatus
intro: Rubus nemorosus subsp. serrulatus, also known as the woodland bramble or serrated woodland blackberry, is a subspecies of Rubus nemorosus, a type of shrub that belongs to the rose family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus nemorosus subsp. serrulatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote digestion, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or salve, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for a range of health benefits.
